# UC-22.22.5 · Biometric SCA Attempt and Liveness Failure Monitoring

## Description

Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PSD2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes payments and sourcetypes psd2:sca, psd2:sca`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=payments`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=psd2:sca`, `sourcetype=psd2:sca``; Lookups: `psd2_sca_baseline`, `psd2_sca_baseline.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="psd2:sca" earliest=-24h
| eval sca_dim="biometric"
| eval challenge_flag=if(challenge="true" OR challenge="1",1,0)
| stats count as authn, sum(eval(outcome="success")) as ok, sum(challenge_flag) as ch, dc(psu_id) as psu by aspsp_id, sca_dim
| lookup psd2_sca_baseline.csv aspsp_id OUTPUT challenge_rate_baseline AS baseline
| eval challenge_rate=round(100*ch/authn,3)
| eval delta=round(challenge_rate-baseline,3)
| eval uc_id="22.22.5"
| where authn>100 AND (delta>5 OR delta<-5)
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**Biometric SCA Attempt and Liveness Failure Monitoring (PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC)** — Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: psd2:sca. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="psd2:sca", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sca_dim** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **challenge_flag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **aspsp_id, sca_dim**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **challenge_rate** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where authn>100 AND (delta>5 OR delta<-5)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: aspsp_id, authn, baseline, ch, challenge_flag, challenge_rate, delta, ok.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with payment switch or bank API logs, fraud case tool output, and the ServiceNow or GRC record for a known test transaction or drill window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (challenge_rate), Bar chart (delta by aspsp_id), Table (top outliers)

## SPL

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="psd2:sca" earliest=-24h
| eval sca_dim="biometric"
| eval challenge_flag=if(challenge="true" OR challenge="1",1,0)
| stats count as authn, sum(eval(outcome="success")) as ok, sum(challenge_flag) as ch, dc(psu_id) as psu by aspsp_id, sca_dim
| lookup psd2_sca_baseline.csv aspsp_id OUTPUT challenge_rate_baseline AS baseline
| eval challenge_rate=round(100*ch/authn,3)
| eval delta=round(challenge_rate-baseline,3)
| eval uc_id="22.22.5"
| where authn>100 AND (delta>5 OR delta<-5)
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta
```
